---
title: "Alias Set-up"
subtitle: "Windows"
date: last-modified
author: Kline DuBose
image: "alia_set_up_image.png"
bibliography: "C:/Users/kline/Desktop/library.bib"
format:
  html: 
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    theme: simplex
execute: 
  warning: false
  echo: true
  cache: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
homework: ["Homework", "Coding", "Tutorial"]
---

The goal of this tutorial is to help me keep track of how to set up alias for Windows Powershell. This will be focused on setting up aliases to access resources at the [University of Utah CHPC](https://chpc.utah.edu/).

# Set-up

Click on the Windows Button and search for "Windows PowerShell". Once this is open, we will find the profile and talk about how to set up the function.

# Finding/Creating your $profile

1. Test to see if you already have a profile document `test-path $profile`
2. If true, you can find the location of your profile with `echo $profile` or you can open the file with `notepad $profile`.
3. If false, we will first create the profile with `New-Item -Type File -Path $profile -Force`
4. After the file is created, open it up using `notepad $profile`.

# Creating the functioin

Once the $profile is open, you can create functions. They should follow this format:

```
Function <name of function> { command to be called }
```

For example, if I wanted an alias that makes it easier to get into notchpeak at the CHPC:

```
Function notchpeak { ssh [uID]@notchpeak.chpc.utah.edu }
```
